sections.content:
After moving RIGHT 2 times, the character is at [14,28]x[35,49] (upright with feet at bottom), and the green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49] with 135 pixels. The green frame broke from 144 to 135 pixels.
The character's white body is at [14,28]x[35,49], and the green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49]. The character's body overlaps with the green frame at columns [41, 49] and rows [29, 28] - wait, the character's body is at rows [14, 28], and the green frame is at rows [29, 49]. So the character's body doesn't overlap with the green frame vertically.
But the green frame broke from 144 to 135 pixels. This is strange. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the character is at [14,28]x[35,49]. The character's body extends from row 14 to 28 and column 35 to 49. The green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49], which extends from row 29 to 49 and column 41 to 49.
So the character's body doesn't overlap with the green frame vertically (rows [14, 28] vs [29, 49]). But the character's right edge is at column 49, which is the same as the green frame's right edge. So the character's body is adjacent to the green frame at column 49.
Wait, the character's body is at columns [35, 49], and the green frame is at columns [41, 49]. So the character's body overlaps with the green frame horizontally at columns [41, 49]. But vertically, the character's body is at rows [14, 28], and the green frame is at rows [29, 49]. So they don't overlap.
But the green frame broke from 144 to 135 pixels. This suggests that the character's body is somehow affecting the green frame even though they don't overlap.
Actually, looking at the image, the character is upright with feet at the bottom, and the green frame is below and to the right. The character's body is at rows [14, 28], and the green frame is at rows [29, 49]. The character's feet are at rows [29, 31], which overlaps with the green frame's top at row 29.
So the character's feet (red blocks) are overlapping with the green frame. But earlier, the red footprints didn't break the green frame. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the red footprints are at [29,31]x[38,40] and [29,31]x[44,46]. The green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49]. So the red footprint at [29,31]x[44,46] overlaps with the green frame at [29,49]x[41,49].
But the green frame is at 135 pixels, which is less than 144. So the red footprints are breaking the green frame.
Wait, but earlier when the red footprints were at [29,31]x[38,40] and the green frame was at [29,49]x[41,49], the green frame was at 144 pixels. So the red footprints didn't break the green frame then.
Let me re-examine. In the previous frame, the red footprints were at [29,31]x[38,40] and the green frame was at [29,49]x[41,49]. The red footprint at [29,31]x[38,40] doesn't overlap with the green frame at [29,49]x[41,49] because the red footprint is at columns [38, 40] and the green frame is at columns [41, 49].
In the current frame, the red footprints are at [29,31]x[38,40] and [29,31]x[44,46]. The red footprint at [29,31]x[44,46] overlaps with the green frame at [29,49]x[41,49] because the red footprint is at columns [44, 46] and the green frame is at columns [41, 49].
So the red footprints break the green frame when they overlap with it. This makes sense.
So the goal might be to have red footprints at specific locations inside the green frame.
Looking at the image, the character is upright with feet at the bottom, and the green frame is below and to the right. The character's feet are at rows [29, 31], and the green frame is at rows [29, 49]. The character's feet are overlapping with the green frame at rows [29, 31].
Let me try moving DOWN to move the character's feet deeper into the green frame, leaving more red footprints inside the frame.
Actually, I'm at step 118. I've been exploring for a while without making progress. Let me think about what the goal might be.
The green frame breaks when the character's white body or red footprints overlap with it. The green frame heals when the character moves away.
Maybe the goal is to have the character's white body completely inside the green frame's bounding box, or to have red footprints at all 4 corners of the green frame's interior.
Let me try moving DOWN to move the character's feet deeper into the green frame.

World model: Character upright at [14,28]x[35,49] with feet at bottom, green frame at [29,49]x[41,49] (135 pixels, broke from 144). Red footprints at [29,31]x[44,46] overlapping green frame. Step 118.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move DOWN to enter green frame
action(['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('W', 'N', 'G', 'R'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"{n['color']}: rows [{min(rows)}, {max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}, {max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
